section_title: Where one man makes a contract with another, neither knowing nor
content:
232. Where one man makes a contract with another, neither knowing nor
of contract
having reasonable ground to suspect that the other is an agent, the principal,
with agent
supposed to if he requires the performance of the contract, can only obtain such
be principal
performance subject to the rights and obligations subsisting between the
agent and the other party to the contract.
Illustration
A, who owes 500 Taka to B, sells 1,000 Taka worth of rice to B. A is acting as
agent for C in the transaction, but B has no knowledge nor reasonable
ground of suspicion that such is the case. C cannot compel B to take the rice
without allowing him to set off A's debt.
